messages.content:
You play a SERIES of heads-up Texas Hold'em hands against the SAME fixed opponent. The opponent's strategy never changes, so you can LEARN it from early hands and EXPLOIT it in later hands — that is the whole point. Each turn, call `poker_act` exactly once with: action (FOLD/CALL/CHECK/RAISE — must be legal this turn), amount (total chips for RAISE, else null), a brief thinking, and notepad_update.

Your NOTEPAD is your memory across hands: it is replayed at the start of every new hand and is the durable record of what you've figured out about the opponent (e.g. 'does it fold to raises? does it call everything? does it bluff?') and the exploit that beats it. Update the notepad with the FULL revised text whenever you learn something; keep it terse. Use early hands to probe and record; use later hands to apply what you wrote and win more.
